question: What is a Large Language Model (LLM)?
common_mistakes: Thinking LLMs possess true reasoning or sentience (they are statistical next-word predictors)

roasts.angry_staff: It's not magic, it's just fancy autocomplete powered by billions of parameters.
roasts.strict_prof: LLMs map sequences of discrete tokens to probability distributions over a vocabulary using self-attention mechanisms.
roasts.faang_bar: Good. Explain the difference between an autoregressive model and a masked language model.
roasts.startup_cto: Just use the OpenAI API. Training your own foundational model will bankrupt us.

question: Explain the core mechanism of the Transformer architecture.
common_mistakes: Thinking Transformers process text sequentially like RNNs (they process tokens in parallel)
followups: Why are positional encodings necessary in Transformers?

roasts.angry_staff: Attention is all you need. Literally the title of the paper. Read it.
roasts.strict_prof: Self-attention computes a weighted sum of values based on queries and keys via dot products.
roasts.faang_bar: Good. Explain the computational complexity of self-attention with respect to sequence length.
roasts.startup_cto: I don't care how it works as long as it writes my marketing copy.

question: Prompt Engineering vs Fine-Tuning vs RAG. When to use which?
common_mistakes: Fine-tuning an LLM just to give it new factual knowledge (RAG is better for this)
followups: Why is fine-tuning bad for updating factual knowledge?

roasts.angry_staff: Stop trying to fine-tune GPT-4 with your company wiki! Use RAG!
roasts.strict_prof: Fine-tuning updates the parametric memory, whereas RAG utilizes non-parametric memory, effectively mitigating knowledge obsolescence.
roasts.faang_bar: Good. Explain how LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) works for fine-tuning.
roasts.startup_cto: Just write a better prompt. Fine-tuning takes too much engineering time.

question: How does RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) work?
common_mistakes: Passing entire documents into the prompt (exceeds context window limit)
followups: What is semantic search vs keyword search?
How do you handle chunking strategies?

roasts.angry_staff: If you don't chunk your documents properly, your RAG system will just return garbage context.
roasts.strict_prof: RAG reduces hallucinations by grounding the generative model's output in retrieved, verifiable facts.
roasts.faang_bar: Good. Explain how you would implement a re-ranking step in a RAG pipeline.
roasts.startup_cto: RAG is the only way to make the AI stop making up lies about our product.

question: What are Vector Embeddings?
common_mistakes: Thinking an embedding contains the actual text data

roasts.angry_staff: King - Man + Woman = Queen. That's embeddings 101. If you don't get that, you're doomed.
roasts.strict_prof: Embeddings map discrete categorical variables to continuous dense vectors in an n-dimensional space.
roasts.faang_bar: Good. How does the choice of embedding model affect your RAG performance?
roasts.startup_cto: Turn all our docs into vectors and shove them in Pinecone. Boom. AI.

question: What is Tokenization in LLMs?
common_mistakes: Assuming 1 token = 1 word (usually 1 token ≈ 0.75 words in English)
followups: Why do LLMs use subword tokenization instead of word-level?

roasts.angry_staff: You can't just pass a raw string into a neural net! It only understands numbers!
roasts.strict_prof: Subword tokenization balances vocabulary size and sequence length, mitigating the out-of-vocabulary problem.
roasts.faang_bar: Good. How does tokenization affect an LLM's ability to perform character-level tasks like spelling or rhyming?
roasts.startup_cto: Watch the token count! Every token costs us money in the API.

question: Explain Temperature and Top-P in LLM generation.
common_mistakes: Using high temperature for tasks requiring factual accuracy
followups: What happens if Temperature is set to 0?
Should you adjust both Temperature and Top-P simultaneously?

roasts.angry_staff: Setting temperature to 1.5 for a code generation task? The AI is just going to hallucinate syntax.
roasts.strict_prof: Temperature scales the logits before the softmax function, flattening or sharpening the probability distribution.
roasts.faang_bar: Good. Contrast Top-P sampling with Top-K sampling.
roasts.startup_cto: Keep temperature at 0 for the customer service bot. I don't want it getting creative with refund policies.

question: What are AI Hallucinations and how can you mitigate them?
common_mistakes: Assuming the LLM is 'lying' rather than just statistically predicting an incorrect sequence
followups: How can you evaluate an LLM's hallucination rate?

roasts.angry_staff: The AI doesn't 'know' anything. It's just guessing the next word. Of course it hallucinates!
roasts.strict_prof: Hallucinations are an inherent byproduct of generative models optimizing for fluency over factual accuracy.
roasts.faang_bar: Good. Discuss the role of RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback) in reducing hallucinations.
roasts.startup_cto: If the AI hallucinates legal advice, we're getting sued. Fix it.

question: What is a Vector Database?
common_mistakes: Thinking a standard SQL database with JSON columns is as fast as a dedicated Vector DB for similarity search
followups: Give examples of Vector Databases (e.g., Pinecone, Milvus, pgvector).
What is an ANN algorithm like HNSW?

roasts.angry_staff: Doing a cosine similarity scan over 10 million rows in Postgres without an index? Enjoy the timeout.
roasts.strict_prof: Vector databases index embeddings using spatial data structures to achieve sub-linear query time complexities.
roasts.faang_bar: Good. Explain the trade-offs between HNSW and IVF-PQ indexing methods.
roasts.startup_cto: Just use Pinecone so we don't have to manage infrastructure.

question: How do you evaluate the performance of an LLM application?
common_mistakes: Relying solely on BLEU or ROUGE scores for complex generative tasks
followups: What are BLEU and ROUGE scores?

roasts.angry_staff: BLEU scores for creative writing? That's completely useless. Use an LLM as a judge.
roasts.strict_prof: Evaluating open-ended generation remains an open research problem, necessitating a multi-faceted approach.
roasts.faang_bar: Good. How do you prevent self-bias when using an LLM to evaluate its own outputs?
roasts.startup_cto: I don't care about MMLU scores. Does it actually solve our customers' problems?
